title: CCRG iter-5 Positioning & Citations: M1 Selective-Unlearning Distinctness (Regime-Scoped), M2 Width/Layer-Dependence of Absorption, Locked Venue Table
summary: Positions the two new iteration-5 results of the Counterfactual Co-Response Grouping (CCRG) paper against the right literatures and finalizes the venue-locked citation set for GEN_PAPER_TEXT. Pure web research, $0, no code; builds on iter-3 (art_i-tkvFCKneA-) and iter-4 (art_QBxBPF-9Ldxe) without re-doing settled entries. THREE deliverables. (A) M1 = a KG-named single-ABSORBER edit claimed to beat a dense diff-of-means / LEACE whole-concept-erasure baseline on a joint within-hierarchy-collateral + fluency metric at matched on-target (forget) effect. VERDICT: DISTINCT but must be framed NARROWLY. The broad claim 'an SAE-feature intervention can beat a dense baseline on unlearning/steering' is CONTESTED, not unprecedented: Farrell/Lau/Conmy (2410.19278, NeurIPS-2024 Safe-GenAI Workshop) report multi-feature SAE unlearning has side-effects >= RMU and that SAE quality must improve to match fine-tuning, and AxBench/Kantamneni concede dense beats SAEs on aggregate -- BUT CRISP (2508.13650, ACL 2026), SAUCE (ICCV 2025, VLM), SSPU (2505.24428, EMNLP 2025) and SRMU (2512.16297) all claim utility-preserving SAE-unlearning wins on WHOLE concepts. The defensible novelty is the CONJUNCTION none combine: (1) regime = single SUB-CONTEXT removal WITH PARENT preservation on the SAME hierarchy (where a dense whole-concept direction structurally over-shoots); (2) unit = ONE KG-NAMED absorber latent DISCOVERED (not pre-known) -- directly answering the 'Use SAEs to Discover, not Act' framing threat (2506.23845, Peng et al.); (3) metric = within-hierarchy sibling+parent collateral mapped onto the established forget-quality/retain-utility/fluency Pareto triad (WMDP=ICML2024, TOFU=COLM2024, MUSE=ICLR2025, RWKU=NeurIPS2024-D&B, SHRED 'new Pareto frontier' 2605.07482, survey 2601.13264). Three adversarial disprove searches returned only whole-concept removal (SAUCE/SAeUron/CRISP/Harry-Potter-ablation) and non-archival single-feature steering near-misses (GDM anger feature; ETH SAE-vs-MeanActDiff) -- no archival precedent for the conjunction. Honest concession + scope guardrail + 'if it fails reframe to auditability' contingency provided. (B) M2 = cross-dictionary (65k-width and/or second-layer) replication is the literature-PREDICTED robustness axis with a SIGNED prediction: SAEBench (2503.09532) states verbatim 'Feature Absorption ... scores degrade at larger widths' and 'Absorption scores worsen with increased dictionary size for all architectures except Matryoshka' and 'Unlearning effectiveness is best at earlier layers and varies significantly by layer' (width series 4k/16k/65k at layers [5,12,19]); Feature Hedging (2505.11756) gives the two-sided law (absorption worse WIDER, hedging worse NARROWER, 'width is not a neutral hyperparameter'); Matryoshka (2503.17547) gives the dictionary-size law + a non-spelling 'Lily/female-names' absorption hole. So 16k->65k should show MORE absorption (the CCRG phenomenon stronger), making replication the expected outcome and non-replication itself a publishable dictionary-dependence finding. Feasibility confirmed: Gemma-Scope has 65k residual SAEs at ALL gemma-2-2b layers (Neuronpedia) + width series at layers 5/12/19; numeric-digit reconstruction <0.9 caveat has NO literature basis -> gate the numeric arm on measured cosine. (C) Citation finalization: carries forward every iter-3/iter-4 lock; adds 13 verified M1/M2 cites with IDs/venues/authors; KEY CORRECTION: SAUCE arXiv 2503.14530 is WITHDRAWN but an ICCV 2025 CVF camera-ready exists -> cite CVF; CRISP UPGRADED to ACL 2026; SSPU=EMNLP 2025; Farrell=NeurIPS 2024 Safe-GenAI Workshop; possible Deng NeurIPS-2025 upgrade flagged (verify track); BibTeX block + corrections diff + extended presentation-strip checklist provided. Outputs research_out.json + research_report.md (sections A-D).
answer:
This iter-5 pass positions CCRG's two new load-bearing results (M1 selective-edit, M2 cross-dictionary) against the literatures the prior audits did NOT cover, and finalizes the venue-locked citation set. All claims are traceable to fetched URLs; no bibliographic field was invented; the load-bearing M1 novelty claim was stress-tested with three adversarial disprove searches.

=== (A) M1 POSITIONING: DISTINCT, BUT FRAME NARROWLY (the honest verdict) ===
M1 claims that a KG-named single ABSORBER edit beats a dense diff-of-means / LEACE whole-concept-erasure baseline on a joint within-hierarchy-collateral + fluency metric at matched on-target effect, in the regime where removing ONE sub-context while PRESERVING the parent is the goal a dense direction over-shoots. The specific claim is DISTINCT and survives disprove searches, but the BROAD claim 'an SAE intervention can beat a dense baseline at unlearning/steering' is CONTESTED, not unprecedented. AGAINST SAEs (the honest concession): Farrell, Lau & Conmy [1] (the canonical SAE-unlearning paper) find that intervening with multiple SAE features 'can unlearn multiple different topics, but with similar or larger unwanted side-effects than the existing Representation Misdirection for Unlearning technique' and conclude 'Current SAE quality or intervention techniques would need to improve to make SAE-based unlearning comparable to the existing fine-tuning based techniques' [1]; AxBench (carried, ICML 2025) finds diff-of-means beats SAEs on detection and simple baselines beat SAE steering; Kantamneni et al. (carried) find no consistent SAE sparse-probing advantage. FOR SAEs (recent, contested): CRISP [4] (ACL 2026) 'outperforms prior approaches' on WMDP while 'preserving general and in-domain capabilities'; SAUCE [5] claims +18.04% unlearning quality at comparable utility (vision-language); SSPU [6] (EMNLP 2025) and SRMU [8] claim interpretable/robust utility-preserving unlearning; a GDM blog [20] reports a single anger SAE feature that Pareto-beats the anger steering vector; an ETH thesis [21] reports SAE steering 'slightly better than MeanActDiff'. Therefore CCRG must NOT claim novelty for 'an SAE intervention can preserve utility / beat dense' in general. The defensible, novel contribution is the CONJUNCTION none of these combine: (1) REGIME -- single SUB-CONTEXT removal WITH PARENT preservation, where target and preserved concept lie on the SAME semantic hierarchy (sibling/parent), the exact case a dense whole-concept direction over-shoots (every precedent removes a WHOLE concept/topic/forget-set and preserves UNRELATED material); (2) UNIT -- ONE KG-NAMED absorber latent, DISCOVERED (not pre-specified) by the two-track grouping + interventional feature-KG, which directly answers Peng et al.'s 'Use SAEs to Discover Unknown Concepts, Not to Act on Known Concepts' [3]; (3) METRIC -- within-hierarchy sibling+parent collateral + fluency, mapped onto the recognised forget-quality / retain-utility / fluency Pareto triad. The decisive DENSE comparator is LEACE [carried, NeurIPS 2023] (a single dense hyperplane removes the WHOLE concept and structurally cannot localize to a sub-context). The eval bar + honest concession source is AxBench [carried]. SCOPE GUARDRAIL: claim the win ONLY in the single-sub-context-with-parent-preservation regime; do NOT claim to beat dense on aggregate whole-concept steering/unlearning. CONTINGENCY: if at experiment time the edit fails to beat dense even in-regime, reframe honestly to 'KG-named localization buys human-auditability and structural parent-preservation, not necessarily a better collateral number' -- still a contribution -- and do not suppress.

M1 metric grounding: on-target effect <-> forget quality (WMDP [9], TOFU [10], MUSE [11]); sibling+parent collateral <-> retain-utility / neighbour-concept preservation (MUSE's utility property [11]; CRISP 'preserve general+in-domain' [4]); fluency/LLM-judge <-> AxBench Concept/Instruct/Fluency [carried] + the survey's metric triad [14]; better on all three at matched forget effect <-> Pareto-dominance on the forget-utility frontier (SHRED 'new Pareto-optimal trade-off' [13]; survey's 'stubborn forget-utility trade-off' [14]). Benchmark venues verified this iteration: WMDP = ICML 2024 [9]; TOFU = COLM 2024 [10]; MUSE = ICLR 2025 Poster [11]; RWKU = NeurIPS 2024 Datasets & Benchmarks Track [12]. The 'Discover-not-Act' threat [3] is RECONCILED (CCRG discovers the absorber via grouping+KG; the win is regime-scoped, not a claim SAEs beat dense on pre-known whole concepts) -- it actually SUPPORTS CCRG's discovery framing. Three adversarial disprove searches (sub-concept-unlearning-preserve-parent; KG-guided-single-feature-edit-beats-diff-of-means; homograph/word-sense-targeted-unlearning) returned only whole-concept removal (SAUCE [5], SAeUron [carried], CRISP [4], Harry-Potter SAE ablation [22]) and non-archival single-feature steering near-misses [20][21]; NO archival precedent for the conjunction.

=== (B) M2 POSITIONING: WIDTH/DICTIONARY/LAYER DEPENDENCE = LITERATURE-PREDICTED ROBUSTNESS AXIS (signed) ===
Cross-dictionary replication is the robustness axis the absorption literature explicitly predicts, WITH a signed expectation (more absorption at 65k). SAEBench [15] states verbatim that 'Feature Absorption, SCR, and TPP scores degrade at larger widths' and 'Absorption scores worsen with increased dictionary size for all architectures except Matryoshka' and 'Unlearning effectiveness is best at earlier layers and varies significantly by layer' -- with the width series trained on widths 4k/16k/65k at layers [5, 12, 19] of Gemma-2-2B (Figure 6 = change 16k->65k). Feature Hedging [16] gives the crisp TWO-SIDED law: absorption is 'more severe the wider the SAE', hedging is 'more severe the narrower the SAE', and 'SAE width is not a neutral hyperparameter'. Matryoshka SAEs [17] give the dictionary-size version ('these problems become more severe as SAEs scale to larger dictionary sizes') and exist BECAUSE absorption scales with size, plus a non-spelling absorption example ('for the name Lily, the latent for female names might instead become an all-female-names-except-Lily latent ... absorption creates specific holes in otherwise intact general features') that corroborates CCRG's name/homograph slice. Triangulated, three independent sources agree absorption INCREASES with width/dictionary-size, and SAEBench adds explicit layer-dependence -- so moving the 16k spine to 65k should make the CCRG phenomenon (recall holes, anchored set-cover absorbers, a-priori router firing) MORE pronounced, not less. BOTH-OUTCOME framing: (i) replication-with-deltas = the auditability spine generalises across dictionaries in the literature-predicted direction; (ii) non-replication = a dictionary-dependence of the CCRG signal, itself publishable, though FLAGGED as the LESS likely outcome given the width law points the other way. Feasibility confirmed (documentation only, no downloads): Neuronpedia hosts a Gemma-2-2b residual-stream 65k suite across ALL layers plus a 16k all-layers suite [18]; Gemma Scope [19] (BlackboxNLP 2024) trains JumpReLU SAEs on 'all layers and sub-layers of Gemma 2 2B'; SAEBench's own width series sits at layers [5, 12, 19] [15] -- so 16k->65k at layer 12 is a direct drop-in and second-layer arms (layer 5 = earlier, where unlearning is reportedly best; or layer 19 = later) are available. NUMERIC-DIGIT CAVEAT: a targeted search found NO published result establishing poor SAE reconstruction on digit/number tokens; treat the numeric arm's <0.9 reconstruction as a CCRG-internal observation and GATE that arm on the measured per-family cosine (>0.9) rather than assume cross-width transfer -- do not assert a literature basis.

=== (C) CITATION FINALIZATION ===
All iter-3/iter-4 venue locks are carried forward verbatim (Chanin 2409.14507 = NeurIPS 2025 Oral; AxBench/SAEBench/Matryoshka/SAeUron/DPE = ICML 2025; CanonicalUnits/PS-Eval = ICLR 2025; SALVE = ICLR 2026 Trustworthy-AI Workshop vision; SCR/TPP = NeurIPS 2024 ATTRIB Workshop; LEACE = NeurIPS 2023; Mind-the-GAP = AISTATS 2024; SAE-TS/SRS/Feature-Hedging/Muchane/SASA/Winnicki/Deng/Kantamneni/CDLC/Chanin-audit = preprints; SAEmnesia/SNCE author lists flagged). THIRTEEN new M1/M2 cites added with verified fields: Farrell 2410.19278 = NeurIPS 2024 Safe GenAI Workshop; CRISP 2508.13650 = ACL 2026 (accepted); Conditional-Clamping 2503.11127 = preprint; SSPU 2505.24428 = EMNLP 2025 Main; SRMU 2512.16297 = preprint; SAUCE 2503.14530 = ICCV 2025 (CVF camera-ready; arXiv WITHDRAWN -> cite CVF); Peng 'Discover-not-Act' 2506.23845 = preprint; WMDP 2403.03218 = ICML 2024; TOFU 2401.06121 = COLM 2024; MUSE 2407.06460 = ICLR 2025 Poster; RWKU 2406.10890 = NeurIPS 2024 D&B; SHRED 2605.07482 = preprint; survey 2601.13264 = preprint. KEY CORRECTION vs plan: SAUCE's arXiv is WITHDRAWN -> use the ICCV 2025 CVF open-access version only. POSSIBLE UPGRADE flagged (not applied): Deng 2506.18141 appears on neurips.cc/virtual/2025/129759 -> may be NeurIPS 2025; verify track before upgrading. Muchane 2506.01197 has an OpenReview forum (C7M6F0OJ1l) but acceptance unconfirmed -> keep preprint. Full BibTeX + corrections diff + extended presentation-strip checklist are in research_report.md.

OVERALL: M1 = DISTINCT but regime-scoped (cite-and-distinguish Farrell/CRISP/SAUCE/SSPU/SRMU/Discover-not-Act/SAE-TS/SRS/LEACE/AxBench; honest concession that dense wins on aggregate; reframe-to-auditability contingency if the in-regime win fails). M2 = literature-PREDICTED robustness axis with a signed prediction (absorption increases 16k->65k; SAEBench/Feature-Hedging/Matryoshka), both outcomes pre-framed, feasibility confirmed. Citations locked + 13 new verified + SAUCE/Deng/Muchane flags resolved or escalated. CONFIDENCE: high for all NEW venue verifications (Farrell, CRISP=ACL2026, SSPU=EMNLP2025, SAUCE=ICCV2025-CVF/arXiv-withdrawn, WMDP=ICML2024, TOFU=COLM2024, MUSE=ICLR2025, RWKU=NeurIPS2024-D&B) and for the SAEBench/Feature-Hedging/Matryoshka width-dependence quotes and Gemma-Scope availability; medium for the Deng-NeurIPS-2025 possible upgrade (track unverified) and Muchane acceptance.

sources.title: Applying Sparse Autoencoders to Unlearn Knowledge in Language Models (Farrell, Lau, Conmy)
sources.summary: THE canonical SAE-unlearning paper + honest concession. WMDP-Bio, gemma-2(-2)b-it; 'negative scaling necessary, zero-ablation ineffective'; multi-feature side-effects 'similar or larger than RMU'; 'SAE quality/intervention would need to improve to match fine-tuning'. Nearest M1 precedent (whole biology TOPIC, not sub-context-with-parent).

sources.title: Safe Generative AI Workshop at NeurIPS 2024 (Farrell venue)
sources.summary: Confirms Farrell 2410.19278 venue = NeurIPS 2024 Safe GenAI Workshop (also OpenReview ZtvRqm6oBu; Conmy Google Scholar 'Safe Generative AI Workshop at NeurIPS 2024, 2024'). Cite as workshop, not main proceedings.

sources.title: Use Sparse Autoencoders to Discover Unknown Concepts, Not to Act on Known Concepts (Peng, Movva, Kleinberg, Pierson, Garg)
sources.summary: Framing threat -> reconciliation. 'SAEs may be less effective for acting on known concepts ... powerful for discovering unknown concepts.' SUPPORTS CCRG framing (CCRG discovers the absorber via grouping+KG; win is regime-scoped). Preprint 2025, no venue.

sources.title: CRISP: Persistent Concept Unlearning via Sparse Autoencoders (Ashuach, Arad, Mueller, Tutek, Belinkov)
sources.summary: VENUE UPGRADE: 'Accepted to ACL 2026'. Parameter-level persistent suppression of MULTIPLE salient SAE features for a WMDP concept; 'preserving general and in-domain capabilities'. Whole-concept; not a KG-named single absorber; not within-hierarchy parent preservation.

sources.title: SAUCE: Selective Concept Unlearning in Vision-Language Models with SAEs (Li, Geng, Zhu, Cai, Lyu, Karray) — ICCV 2025 CVF
sources.summary: CORRECTION: arXiv 2503.14530 is WITHDRAWN ('more comparative experiments needed'); an ICCV 2025 CVF camera-ready EXISTS — cite CVF. 'modify selected SAE features to suppress the target concept while preserving other concepts.' Vision-language modality distinguisher; whole-concept among 60; preserves UNRELATED, not same-hierarchy parent.

sources.title: Model Unlearning via Sparse Autoencoder Subspace Guided Projections / SSPU (Wang, Li, Wang, Hu, Zou)
sources.summary: EMNLP 2025 Main (aclanthology 2025.emnlp-main.1348). Builds 'relevant/irrelevant' subspaces from forget-correlated SAE features (QR decomp), constrained param update 'preserving retained knowledge'. Whole-topic; preserves UNRELATED retain set; not a single absorber.

sources.title: Don't Forget It! Conditional Sparse Autoencoder Clamping Works for Unlearning (Khoriaty, Shportko, Mercier, Wood-Doughty)
sources.summary: Northwestern; preprint 2025. Conditional clamping of WMDP-related SAE features on gemma-2-2b; reduces harmful-QA while preserving utility. Whole-domain WMDP; conditional clamp, not single-absorber sub-context edit.

sources.title: Feature-Selective Representation Misdirection for Machine Unlearning / SRMU (Chen, Huang, Choo, Chen)
sources.summary: Preprint 2025. 'Selective Representation Misdirection for Unlearning (SRMU)' — feature-aware, directionally controlled perturbations under ENTANGLED forget/retain; preserves benign utility. Whole forget-set; not parent-preserving sub-context recovery; no KG-named absorber.

sources.title: The WMDP Benchmark: Measuring and Reducing Malicious Use with Unlearning (Li et al.) — ICML 2024
sources.summary: Venue confirmed ICML 2024 (icml.cc/virtual/2024/poster/32695); arXiv 2403.03218. 3,668-question forget-domain proxy with retain-utility split — the forget/retain benchmark convention CCRG's on-target<->forget metric maps onto.

sources.title: TOFU: A Task of Fictitious Unlearning for LLMs (Maini, Feng, Schwarzschild, Lipton, Kolter) — COLM 2024
sources.summary: Venue = COLM 2024 (First Conference on Language Modeling; cited as 'First Conference On Language Modeling, 2024' via openreview B41hNBoWLo); arXiv 2401.06121. 200 fictitious-author profiles; forget-quality vs model-utility metric suite.

sources.title: MUSE: Machine Unlearning Six-Way Evaluation for Language Models (Shi et al.) — ICLR 2025 Poster
sources.summary: Venue confirmed: 'Published 22 Jan 2025 ... ICLR 2025 Poster'; arXiv 2407.06460. Six desirable properties incl. UTILITY PRESERVATION on the retain set — the retain-utility axis for CCRG's collateral metric.

sources.title: RWKU: Benchmarking Real-World Knowledge Unlearning (Jin et al.) — NeurIPS 2024 D&B Track
sources.summary: Venue = NeurIPS 2024 Datasets & Benchmarks Track; arXiv 2406.10890. Real-world knowledge unlearning benchmark; part of the established forget-utility eval ecosystem.

sources.title: SHRED: Retain-Set-Free Unlearning via Self-Distillation with Logit Demotion (Hu, Godbole, Wei, Rostami, Thomason, Jia)
sources.summary: Preprint 2026 (USC). Verbatim: 'establishes a new Pareto-optimal trade-off between forget efficacy and model utility'; 'SHRED sets a new Pareto frontier on the forgetting-utility tradeoff on all four benchmarks' (TOFU, MUSE, RWKU, Hubble). Canonical statement of the forget-vs-utility Pareto frame CCRG borrows.

sources.title: Unlearning in LLMs: Methods, Evaluation, and Open Challenges (Lizzo & Heck)
sources.summary: Preprint 2026 survey. Categorizes data-/parameter-/architecture-centric/hybrid methods; reviews the evaluation ecosystem around 'forgetting effectiveness, knowledge retention, and robustness' (the metric triad CCRG's M1 maps onto).

sources.title: SAEBench (Karvonen et al.) — absorption-vs-width + layer-dependence (full-text grep)
sources.summary: STRONGEST M2 anchor (ICML 2025). Verbatim: 'Feature Absorption, SCR, and TPP scores degrade at larger widths'; 'Absorption scores worsen with increased dictionary size for all architectures except Matryoshka'; 'Unlearning effectiveness is best at earlier layers and varies significantly by layer'. Width series 4k/16k/65k at Gemma-2-2B layers [5,12,19]; Figure 6 = change 16k->65k.

sources.title: Feature Hedging (Chanin, Dulka, Garriga-Alonso) — two-sided width law (full-text grep)
sources.summary: Preprint 2025. Verbatim: absorption 'more severe the wider the SAE'; hedging 'more severe the narrower the SAE'; 'SAE width is not a neutral hyperparameter: narrower SAEs suffer more from hedging than wider SAEs.' Table 1 contrasts absorption (worse wider, requires hierarchy) vs hedging (worse narrower, requires correlation). Signed prediction for 16k->65k.

sources.title: Matryoshka Sparse Autoencoders (Bussmann et al.) — dictionary-size law + Lily example (full-text grep)
sources.summary: ICML 2025. 'as dictionary size increases ... features to be split or absorbed'; 'these problems become more severe as SAEs scale to larger dictionary sizes'; mitigation exists BECAUSE absorption scales with size. Non-spelling example: 'for the name Lily, the latent for female names might instead become an all-female-names-except-Lily latent ... absorption creates specific holes' — corroborates CCRG's name/homograph slice.

sources.title: Gemma-2-2b Residual Stream — 65k All Layers (Neuronpedia)
sources.summary: Feasibility: a Gemma-2-2b residual-stream 65k SAE suite exists across ALL layers (plus 16k all-layers). Confirms the 16k->65k swap at layer 12 (CCRG spine) is a direct drop-in and other layers are available for a second-layer arm. Documentation read only (no download).

sources.title: Gemma Scope: Open SAEs Everywhere All At Once on Gemma 2 (Lieberum et al.) — BlackboxNLP 2024
sources.summary: 'an open suite of JumpReLU SAEs trained on all layers and sub-layers of Gemma 2 2B and 9B and select layers of Gemma 2 27B.' Grounds availability of canonical residual-stream SAEs at layer 12 width 65k and other layers.

sources.title: GDM Mech Interp Team Progress Update #1 (single SAE anger feature Pareto-improvement)
sources.summary: Adversarial disprove near-miss: 'a single SAE feature for anger which is a Pareto-improvement over the anger steering vector from existing work.' WHOLE concept; NON-archival blog; not sub-context/parent; not KG-named. Does NOT defeat M1's conjunction.

sources.title: Comparing SAE Representations and Mean Activation Differences (ETH thesis)
sources.summary: Adversarial disprove near-miss: 'SAE steering achieves slightly better performance than MeanActDiff, especially with instruction-tuned models.' WHOLE concept; non-archival; not sub-context-with-parent-preservation. Does NOT defeat M1.

sources.title: Sparse Autoencoder Feature Ablation for Unlearning (LessWrong)
sources.summary: Adversarial disprove near-miss: SAE feature ablation to unlearn Harry Potter knowledge in Gemma-2-2b. WHOLE-concept removal preserving unrelated performance; non-archival; not a KG-named single-absorber sub-context edit.

sources.title: SSPU EMNLP 2025 Main (anthology) + Farrell citation context
sources.summary: Confirms SSPU venue = EMNLP 2025 Main; also cites 'Eoin Farrell, Yeu-Tong Lau, and Arthur Conmy. 2024. ... In NeurIPS Safe [Generative AI Workshop]', corroborating the Farrell workshop venue.

sources.title: Sparse Feature Coactivation Reveals (Composable/Causal) Semantic Modules (Deng et al.) — NeurIPS 2025 virtual page
sources.summary: POSSIBLE UPGRADE FLAG: Deng 2506.18141 (carried as preprint) appears on a NeurIPS 2025 virtual page — may be NeurIPS 2025; verify track (main vs workshop) at bib-time before upgrading. Carry-forward Axis-1 observational-coactivation near-miss.

sources.title: Incorporating Hierarchical Semantics in SAE Architectures (Muchane et al.) — OpenReview
sources.summary: Upgrade re-check: OpenReview forum exists but acceptance NOT confirmed; Google Scholar still 'arXiv preprint arXiv:2506.01197, 2025'. Keep as preprint (carry-forward). Hierarchical SAE mitigating absorption — supports the M2 'mitigation-exists-because-absorption-scales' narrative.

sources.title: Interpretable LLM Guardrails via Sparse Representation Steering / SRS (He et al.) — carry-forward
sources.summary: Carry-forward (preprint 2025): contrasts pos/neg prompt pairs, bidirectional KL to locate dimensions for a target ATTRIBUTE (safety/fairness/truthfulness). Attribute-level guardrail, NOT a parent-preserving sub-context absorber edit — M1 cite-and-distinguish.

sources.title: LEACE: Perfect Linear Concept Erasure in Closed Form (Belrose et al.) — NeurIPS 2023 (carry-forward)
sources.summary: The decisive DENSE comparator: a single closed-form hyperplane erases the WHOLE concept direction and structurally cannot localize to a sub-context — exactly what CCRG's M1 sub-concept edit is contrasted against.

sources.title: SAEs Are Good for Steering – If You Select the Right Features (EMNLP 2025 Main)
sources.summary: Context for the contested 'SAE-vs-dense' steering picture: SAE steering can work given correct feature selection (measured via counterfactual token-prediction effect). Reinforces that 'SAE intervention beats dense' is contested, not novel — supports scoping M1 narrowly.

follow_up_questions: Should iter-6 add a small WMDP-style sub-domain unlearning demo (or a TOFU/MUSE-style forget-quality vs retain-utility curve) so the M1 selective-edit win lands inside a RECOGNIZED unlearning benchmark, rather than only on the bespoke Georgia/Jordan homograph + first-letter sub-contexts — and is the within-hierarchy sibling+parent collateral the right 'retain' axis to report against those benchmarks' utility metrics?

Given that CRISP (ACL 2026), SAUCE (ICCV 2025), SSPU (EMNLP 2025) and a GDM blog already claim utility-preserving SAE-intervention wins, is one dedicated rebuttal paragraph for Peng et al.'s 'Discover-not-Act' (2506.23845) sufficient, or should the paper foreground the three-part conjunction (sub-context+parent / KG-named absorber / within-hierarchy collateral) as the explicit novelty boundary in the abstract to pre-empt 'this has been done' reviews?

Should the M2 experiment run BOTH a wider dictionary (16k->65k at layer 12) AND a second layer (5 or 19), or is one axis enough — given SAEBench reports absorption worsens with width AND varies by layer (best/earliest-layer unlearning), the two arms test different predictions and a 2x2 (layer x width) would isolate which axis drives any non-replication?

Deng 2506.18141 appears on a NeurIPS 2025 virtual page (title still 'Composable') while the arXiv title is 'Causal Semantic Modules' — should bib-time resolve the venue/track and title mismatch (NeurIPS 2025 main vs workshop) before upgrading from preprint, and likewise confirm Muchane 2506.01197 acceptance (OpenReview C7M6F0OJ1l) and the SAEmnesia/SNCE author lists?

The numeric-digit <0.9 reconstruction caveat has NO literature basis — should iter-6 measure per-family reconstruction cosine across widths and, if the numeric arm fails the gate at 65k, drop it from the cross-dictionary claim and lean on spelling + taxonomic/homograph arms, or instead report the digit-reconstruction limitation as a small standalone finding about SAE coverage of numeral tokens?
